draft.introduction: Psalm 37 is a wisdom psalm addressing the perennial problem of why the wicked prosper. Its acrostic structure and proverbial counsel urge trust in divine justice over anxiety about present injustice.

translation.text: Fret not yourself because of evildoers; be not envious of wrongdoers! For they will soon fade like the grass and wither like the green herb. Trust in the Lord, and do good; dwell in the land and befriend faithfulness. Delight yourself in the Lord, and he will give you the desires of your heart.
translation.original_text: μὴ παραζήλου ἐν πονηρευομένοις μηδὲ ζήλου τοὺς ποιοῦντας τὴν ἀνομίαν

translator_notes.note: The promise that God will give “the desires of your heart” (τὰ αἰτήματα τῆς καρδίας σου) is conditional on delight in God—suggesting that right orientation transforms desire itself, so that what one wants aligns with what God gives.
